rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/18/2020: The following issues remain unaddressed: The small estate affidavits signed by the 4 distributees have the following defects: 1) Paragraph 9 of each affidavit needs to state that no other person has a "superior" right. rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Authority To Engage In Couseling And Execute Any Documents Necessary To Obtain A Reverse Mortgage And To Execute Note And Deed Of Trust/Mortgage/Security Instrument And Loan Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court is to determine whether proposed reverse mortgage should be approved, and if so, on what conditions. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST INITIAL AND LAST NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERNEST LUBBE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/18/2020 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) The petitioner's request for reimbursement of funeral expenses in the amount of $4,914.46 is a creditor's claim under Probate Code section 9000(a)(3) and must be approved as such by the Court under section 9252. 2) If notice of proposed action was not given, did the beneficiaries consent to the donation/abandonment of the tangible personal property? Also, the donation/abandonment must be reported as a loss and taken into account for purposes of calculating the statutory compensation. 3) How many hours were spent on "research regarding grounds for special administration" on 01/08/2019? 4) How many hours were spent on "review local rules" on 01/09/2019? 5) How many hours were spent on "travel to court" and "travel from court" on 01/15/2019?

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROYAL IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Shashona Holmons Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Parties to appear and address the following issues: What is Shashona Holmon's standing to bring a Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings when she is not a trustee or beneficiary of the Royal Trust? Assuming that Ms. Holmon is heir to deceased Royal Trust trustee Sheree Jones, how does this status give her standing to defend the Royal Trust in these proceedings? Petitioner alleges in the opposition to the motion that she is both an heir of her late father's estate and a beneficiary of the Marcelino P. and Dora Lee Tapia Family Trust, and thereby has standing to challenge the Royal Trust as an interested party and a creditor. The Petition, however, does not allege that Petitioner is a beneficiary or trustee of the Marcelino P. and Dora Lee Tapia Family Trust, or of the Royal Trust. It likewise does not allege that Petitioner is a beneficiary or heir of Mrs. Tapia's estate. How does Petitioner meet the definition of an "interested person" under Probate Code section 48, especially in view of the narrow holding of Barefoot v. Jennings (2020) 8 Cal.5th 822? Both parties to address why mediation was not completed by January 24, 2020 in accordance with the December 11, 2019 Minute Order Referring Case to Pro Bono Mediation, and the current status of the court-ordered mediation. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE PARK L. LOUGHLIN LIVING TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Trust'S Title To Real Property (Heggstad Petition)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/25/2020. 1) Notice must be given on a mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115 in compliance with Probate Code section 851(c). 2) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed and served: A) True and correct copies of the two amendments referred to in paragraph 1 of the petition must be provided. B) True and correct copies of the two deeds as well as the deed executed following the closing of the refinance transaction referred to in paragraph 7 of the petition must be provided. C) The notary acknowledgment included in Exhibit A did not comply with Civil Code section 1189(a)(1). D) Should notice be given to the Attorney General? Probate Code sections 851(b)(3) & 17203(a)(3). E) Did Christopher Ragland Loughlin consent to notice given to the P.O. Box address? Probate Code sections 1220(a)(3) & 17100.
